title: University of Calgary Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in Political Science: Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Canada
summary: The University of Calgary is inviting applications for the Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science , an outstanding research opportunity for early-career scholars seeking to advance their academic careers at one of Canada s leading research-intensive universities. Hosted by the Department of Political Science within the Faculty of Arts , this prestigious fellowship provides postdoctoral researchers with the opportunity to conduct high-quality research, collaborate with renowned faculty members, contribute to teaching and academic life, and expand their professional networks in an internationally recognized academic environment. The fellowship is designed to support exceptional scholars who have recently completed their doctoral studies and wish to pursue innovative research in political science. Through dedicated financial support, mentorship, and access to world-class research facilities, the program aims to foster academic excellence while preparing fellows for successful careers in higher education, research institutions, and public policy organizations. Application Deadline: Please refer to the official University of Calgary job posting for the closing date and submit your application before the position closes. About the Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship The Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship honors the legacy of Mark Grosjean by supporting promising scholars whose research contributes to advancing political science. The Department of Political Science at the University of Calgary is recognized for producing impactful research across a wide range of political science disciplines, including comparative politics, Canadian politics, international relations, political theory, public policy, political economy, and quantitative political analysis. As part of the Faculty of Arts, fellows become members of an active academic community that encourages interdisciplinary collaboration, innovation, and public engagement. The fellowship provides an ideal platform for researchers to strengthen their publication record, develop new research projects, secure future grants, and establish themselves within the international political science community. What the Fellowship Offers Successful applicants will benefit from numerous professional and academic opportunities, including: A postdoctoral appointment at the University of Calgary. Access to world-class research facilities and academic resources. Collaboration with experienced faculty members and researchers. Opportunities to present research through seminars and conferences. Professional mentorship and career development. Participation in departmental academic activities. Opportunities to publish research in leading academic journals. An intellectually stimulating and collaborative research environment. The fellowship is designed not only to support independent research but also to encourage meaningful engagement with students, faculty members, and the wider academic community. Research Environment at the University of Calgary The University of Calgary consistently ranks among Canada s leading research universities. The Department of Political Science offers expertise across numerous research fields, providing fellows with opportunities to collaborate on interdisciplinary projects and contribute to research that informs public policy, governance, democratic institutions, and international affairs. Researchers benefit from: Modern research infrastructure. Extensive digital and physical library collections. Collaborative research centres. International research partnerships. Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration. Professional development resources. This environment allows fellows to pursue ambitious research agendas while developing valuable academic leadership skills. Who Should Apply? The fellowship is intended for outstanding early-career researchers who have recently completed a PhD in Political Science or a closely related discipline. Ideal applicants include scholars whose research interests align with areas such as: Comparative Politics Canadian Politics International Relations Public Policy Political Theory Political Economy Democratic Governance Electoral Politics Public Administration Quantitative Political Science Political Behaviour Applicants should demonstrate a strong record of academic achievement, high-quality research, publications, conference presentations, and the ability to contribute meaningfully to the department s research community. Why This Fellowship Matters Securing a postdoctoral fellowship is an important milestone in an academic career. Beyond providing dedicated time for research, postdoctoral appointments help scholars develop independent research portfolios, strengthen publication records, secure competitive grants, expand international collaborations, and prepare for faculty positions. The Mark Grosjean Postdoctoral Fellowship offers researchers an opportunity to work within a supportive academic environment while contributing to research that addresses important political and societal challenges. Whether examining democratic governance, public institutions, global politics, or public policy, fellows are encouraged to produce research that advances knowledge and informs decision-making. Living and Working in Calgary Located in Alberta, Canada, Calgary is known for its high quality of life, vibrant research community, and strong academic institutions. Fellows benefit from living in a multicultural city with excellent infrastructure, access to nature, and numerous professional networking opportunities. The University of Calgary provides an inclusive and welcoming environment that values diversity, equity, and academic excellence, making it an attractive destination for researchers from around the world. How to Apply Interested candidates should prepare all required application documents as outlined in the official job advertisement . Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the eligibility requirements, required qualifications, and application instructions before submitting their materials. Applications should be submitted through the University of Calgary Careers portal . Application Deadline: Applicants should submit their applications before the closing date indicated on the official vacancy announcement.
